headline: An unverifiable claim, made by the party it benefits, is not evidence.
body: This is the spine. We arrived at it three separate times from three different directions: a stated purpose does not unlock a content gate, a weak similarity does not justify a link, and a source you control yourself is not a source. Each time the answer was the same — so we stopped treating it as a rule and started treating it as the thesis.

headline: We would rather show you no citation than a forged one.
body: Every extracted fact quotes the sentence it came from — and we CHECK that the quote is really in your source. Language models paraphrase, and a paraphrased citation is a forgery that looks right. If we cannot find the quote, we drop it and lower our confidence. A missing receipt is honest. A convincing fake is not.

headline: A forced link is worse than an orphan.
body: Disconnected facts are merely less useful. An invented edge is a lie the graph tells itself — it LOOKS like knowledge and is not. So if a new fact does not genuinely connect to what you already know, we connect it to nothing and say so. 'Is related to' is a non-link wearing the costume of a link.

headline: Export is a right. Distribution is a privilege.
body: Your graph is a plain text file on your machine, and we will never withhold it from you — whatever it contains. But when WE are the one carrying your content to someone else, we are a participant in that delivery, and we take responsibility for what we carry. We gate only what we carry.

headline: A private graph makes no claim on anybody.
body: Think privately. Be wrong privately. Change your mind privately. Your own graph needs no permission, no account, and no provenance — because it makes no claim on anyone else. Provenance is a claim made TO SOMEONE; alone with your notes, there is nobody to substantiate it to, and no reason to pretend otherwise.

headline: We will not describe a control we do not have.
body: Our safety documentation states plainly which protections exist and which do not, and an automated check verifies them against the live code every week — recording the failures too. We have already caught ourselves overclaiming, and corrected it in public. A safety promise you cannot verify is just marketing.
